documentLocal: Certified certificates issued by the Registrar (Registered Office; Directors and secretary; Good Standing; Capital)
summary: Cyprus has no single consolidated register extract. The DRCIP instead issues discrete certified certificates that together evidence the current registered structure (registered office, directors/secretary, capital, good standing). Each is an official document obtainable via e-filing or by hand/post, reflecting the company structure as notified to the register up to its date of issue.

evidence.quote: Certificate of Registered Office Address, Certificate of Directors and secretary, Certificate of Shareholders/members, Certificate of Capital, Certificate of Good Standing
evidence.note: Full enumerated list of certificates the Registrar issues.

evidence.quote: the certified copy/certificate shall reflect the structure of the company on the basis of what has been notified to the companies register up to its date of issue

caveats: INFERENCE: no single German-style 'extract' exists in CY; the base document is a bundle of separate certified certificates, so which certificate satisfies REGISTERAUSZUG depends on the KYC data point required.
The certificate reflects only what has been NOTIFIED to the register up to its issue date (potential staleness).

documentLocal: Certificate of Shareholders/members (certified) / Annual Return HE32
summary: The Registrar issues a certified Certificate of Shareholders/members showing name and address of each member, share class/value and number of shares. The Annual Return (Form HE32), filed on the public register, also lists the members/shareholders. Both are official obtainable documents evidencing the REGISTERED (legal) shareholders.

evidence.note: Listed among certificates the Registrar issues; outlines shareholder name/address, share class (value) and number of shares.

evidence.basis: Companies Law Cap. 113 — annual return provisions (Form HE32); DRCIP
evidence.note: Every company must file an annual return (Form HE32) on the public register giving details of shareholders, directors, secretary, registered office and share capital, with audited financial statements.

caveats: CRITICAL nominee/beneficial distinction: the Certificate of Shareholders and the HE32 show only the REGISTERED (legal) shareholders. Cyprus widely uses nominee shareholders holding under declarations of trust; the registered member may be a nominee, not the beneficial owner.
Beneficial owners are held in the separate UBO Register, NOT publicly accessible — only competent authorities and AML-obliged entities; not obtainable as an open registry document.
Annual-return STALENESS: HE32 is a periodic snapshot (filed ~annually after the AGM) and may not reflect intra-year share transfers.

documentLocal: Memorandum and Articles of Association (certified copy)
summary: The company's constitution is the Memorandum and Articles of Association, filed with the Registrar on incorporation. The DRCIP issues a certified copy of the Memorandum and Articles of Association as an official document (by hand/post; not among the online e-filing self-service set).

evidence.note: Listed among the certified copies the Registrar issues.

caveats: UNVERIFIED: certified M&A copy appears obtainable by hand/post rather than via the online e-filing self-service list; delivery channel not fully confirmed from primary text.
A certified copy reflects the constitution as amended up to its date of issue.

documentLocal: Certified certificates issued by the Registrar (Registered Office; Directors and secretary; Good Standing; Capital)
summary: Same DRCIP regime applies to public companies: no single consolidated extract, but discrete certified certificates (registered office, directors/secretary, capital, good standing) that together evidence the current registered structure, obtainable via e-filing or hand/post and reflecting what was notified to the register up to the issue date.

evidence.quote: Certificate of Registered Office Address, Certificate of Directors and secretary, Certificate of Shareholders/members, Certificate of Capital, Certificate of Good Standing
evidence.note: Registrar certificate list applies to companies generally under Cap. 113.

caveats: INFERENCE: DRCIP certificate documentation does not differentiate certificate types by legal form; assumed identical for public companies.
Reflects only filings notified up to the certificate's issue date (staleness).

documentLocal: Certificate of Shareholders/members (certified) / Annual Return HE32
summary: As for private companies, the Registrar issues a certified Certificate of Shareholders/members and the public Annual Return (HE32) lists members, both evidencing the registered (legal) shareholders of a public company under Cap. 113.

evidence.note: Registrar certificate list; annual return HE32 required of every Cap. 113 company.

caveats: CRITICAL nominee/beneficial distinction: certificate/HE32 show REGISTERED (legal) members only; nominee holdings under declaration of trust are common and the beneficial owner sits in the separate, non-public UBO Register.
For a listed/public company, freely traded shares can make the register a materially stale snapshot; beneficial holdings via nominees/custodians are not shown.
INFERENCE: public-company treatment assumed identical to private per the single DRCIP certificate list.

documentLocal: Memorandum and Articles of Association (certified copy)
summary: A public company's constitution is likewise its Memorandum and Articles of Association, filed with the Registrar and obtainable as a certified copy from the DRCIP.

evidence.note: Certified copy issued by the Registrar; applies to companies under Cap. 113 generally.

caveats: INFERENCE: public-company treatment assumed identical to private per the single DRCIP certified-copy list.
Certified copy reflects the constitution as amended up to its date of issue.
